data.details: no commits — worker claimed work but committed nothing

data.summary: Implemented deterministic lexical-tie-break topoSort with cycle detection plus vitest tests covering linear chain, diamond, and cycle; verified via tsx and strict tsc.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/topoSort.test.ts | 25 +++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/topoSort.ts      | 48 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 73 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/topoSort.ts exports topoSort returning a valid deterministic (lexical tie-break) ordering of all nodes; throws with "cycle" in the message on a cyclic graph: topoSort is exported, implements Kahn's algorithm building nodes from all edge endpoints, re-sorts the ready queue lexically before each emission (deterministic lexical tie-break), and throws `topoSort: graph has a cycle (...)` when result.length !== nodes.size, which fires exactly on cyclic graphs since only cycles leave nodes with nonzero in-degree unprocessed. Error message contains "cycle".
✓ calibration/topoSort.test.ts covers a linear chain, a diamond (a first / d last / predecessors before d), and a cycle throwing: Three tests present: linear chain a→b→c asserts exact ordering; diamond asserts order[0]==='a', last==='d', and both b and c indexed before d; cycle test asserts topoSort([[a,b],[b,a]]) throws matching /cycle/.

data.summary: Implemented parseDuration (ms/s/m/h/d, multi-unit, case-insensitive, fail-closed) plus full vitest coverage; all acceptance cases pass, full suite green, no scope deviations.
Implemented RFC-4180 parseCsvLine parser + vitest test (6 cases, all passing) committed in scope.

Implemented semverCompare with full semver 2.0.0 §11 precedence (numeric core, prerelease<release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter) plus a vitest suite covering all six required cases; tests pass, typecheck and baseline npm test green.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/parseDuration.test.ts | 42 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseDuration.ts      | 58 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 100 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseDuration.ts exports parseDuration returning ms: Named export parseDuration(input: string): number returns summed milliseconds via UNIT_MS table.
✓ handles ms/s/m/h/d units: UNIT_MS covers all five units with correct millisecond factors (ms=1, s=1000, m=60000, h=3600000, d=86400000); TOKEN_RE orders ms before s/m so '500ms' binds correctly.
✓ multi-unit support: Loop over TOKEN_RE.exec sums all tokens; tests verify '1h30m'=5400000 and '2d4h'.
✓ case-insensitive: TOKEN_RE has the i flag and unit lookup lowercases; tests verify '1H30M' and '500MS'.
✓ whitespace tolerated: \s* between number and unit, between-token gaps accepted if whitespace-only, leading/trailing whitespace passes the trim checks; tests cover '2d 4h' and '  1h 30m  '.
✓ throws on empty input: trim()==='' check throws; tests cover '' and '   '.
✓ throws on unknown unit: '5x' matches no token, so the trailing-leftover check throws; test present.
✓ throws on negative: NEGATIVE_RE rejects a minus sign before digits; test covers '-5s'.
✓ test file covers 90s, 1h30m, 500ms, 2d, case-insensitivity, and two throwing cases: All four value cases asserted with exact ms, case-insensitivity tested, and three throwing cases (empty, unknown unit, negative) — exceeds the required two.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts | 28 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseCsvLine.ts      | 45 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 73 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine handling quoted fields, escaped "" quotes, embedded commas inside quotes, empty fields, and empty input: The diff adds calibration/parseCsvLine.ts with an exported parseCsvLine. The state machine opens quotes only at field start, treats commas inside quotes as literal (inQuotes branch appends ch), handles doubled "" as a literal quote (lookahead + i++ skip), produces empty fields via cur reset on comma with the final fields.push(cur) covering trailing/empty cases, and empty input falls through the loop to push "" yielding [""]. Tracing each test input through the code confirms the expected outputs.
✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts covers plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases: The test file contains all five required cases with correct expected values: "a,b,c" → [a,b,c]; '"a,b",c' → ["a,b","c"]; '"she said ""hi""",x' → ['she said "hi"',"x"]; "a,,c" → [a,"",c]; "" → [""], plus a trailing-comma case.

1 commit(s):
calibration/semverCompare.test.ts | 28 +++++++++++++++++
 calibration/semverCompare.ts      | 66 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 94 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare returning -1|0|1 with correct numeric core + prerelease precedence (prerelease < release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter): Exported function returns only -1|0|1 (sign() + compareIdent() are typed -1|0|1 and all return paths route through them). Core major.minor.patch compared numerically per index. §11.3 handled: empty-pre vs non-empty-pre returns +1/-1 so prerelease < release. compareIdent: numeric identifiers compared numerically, numeric<alphanumeric (an→-1, bn→1), alphanumeric compared ASCII-lexically. Equal shared prefix falls through to sign(length diff), so more identifiers outrank fewer (§11.4.4). Build metadata stripped before comparison (§10).
✓ calibration/semverCompare.test.ts covers core ordering, equality, prerelease-vs-release, and two prerelease-identifier cases: Tests present: core ordering (1.0.0 vs 1.0.1, and 1.2.0 vs 1.1.9 crossing minor/patch), exact equality (1.0.0 vs 1.0.0 → 0), prerelease-vs-release (1.0.0-alpha < 1.0.0), and two prerelease-identifier cases: numeric<alphanumeric (alpha.1 < alpha.beta) and longer-outranks-shorter (alpha.1 > alpha). All assert exact -1|0|1 values and match spec-correct expectations.

data.summary: Implemented stack-based rpnEval (+,-,*,/ over ints/decimals with unknown-token/underflow/leftover throws) plus 6 passing tests, committed as 2d5dfac.

Implemented capacity-bounded lruCache (get/set/size) with recency refreshed by both get and set, plus 7 passing vitest cases covering plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/rpnEval.test.ts | 28 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/rpnEval.ts      | 41 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 69 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval evaluating +,-,*,/ via a stack over ints/decimals; throws on unknown token, operand underflow, and leftover operands. calibration/rpnEval.test.ts covers four valid expressions and two throwing (underflow + leftover) cases.: rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval using a number[] stack with all four operators in OPS; NUM_RE accepts ints and decimals (optional negative sign); throws Error on unknown token (NUM_RE mismatch), operand underflow (stack.length < 2 at operator), and leftover operands (stack.length !== 1 at end). The test file contains exactly four passing-value tests (3 4 + = 7, 3 4 + 5 * = 35, 10 2 / = 5, 2 3 4 * + = 14) and two throwing tests: underflow ('3 +') and leftover ('1 2 3 +').

1 commit(s):
calibration/lruCache.test.ts | 76 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/lruCache.ts      | 42 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 118 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/lruCache.ts exports lruCache with get/set/size, capacity-bounded, recency refreshed by BOTH get and set, in-place update doesn't grow size: lruCache is exported with get/set/size. Eviction on set when map.size >= capacity keeps it capacity-bounded. get refreshes recency via delete+re-set on hit; set refreshes recency for existing keys via delete before re-insert (in-place update path deletes first, so size never grows). Map insertion-order iteration makes keys().next() the LRU entry — implementation is correct.
✓ calibration/lruCache.test.ts covers plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound: Test 1 covers plain eviction (a,b,c at capacity 2 evicts a). Test 2 covers get-refreshing recency (get a, then set c evicts b, a survives). Test 4 asserts size() ≤ capacity across 10 inserts at capacity 3 and exact final size. Additional tests cover in-place update size invariance, set-refreshes-recency, missing-key get, and capacity validation.
